text:
Fake proverb: "The crow that builds its nest in borrowed trees will find itself homeless when the wind changes."

Explanation: This proverb warns against relying on external circumstances or resources you don't truly own or control. The crow represents anyone building their life on unstable ground—temporary arrangements that feel permanent until they suddenly collapse. The changing wind symbolizes the inevitable shifts in circumstances that everyone faces. The proverb teaches that true security comes from building on solid foundations you own and control, rather than becoming comfortable in borrowed situations. It speaks to the importance of self-sufficiency and long-term thinking, suggesting that shortcuts and temporary advantages are eventually revealed as insufficient when conditions change.
